title: Simulator REST API has no authentication
description:
**Locations:**
- python/src/lichen/sim/server.py:30-45 (security note in docstring)
- python/src/lichen/sim/api.py (entire module)

**Risk:** The simulator REST API allows full control over simulations: creating/deleting nodes, applying chaos rules (dropping packets, partitioning networks), and advancing time. There is no authentication or authorization.

**Documented mitigation:** The docstring and CLI help say to only bind to 127.0.0.1. However, `--bind-address` accepts any value including 0.0.0.0, and users may do this for Docker/VM access.

**Maintainer burden:** When users expose the API and get their simulations disrupted, they'll file issues. Adding at least a simple API key or warning when binding to non-localhost would reduce support burden.

**Current state:** Security note exists in docstring (line 40-44) and CLI help (line 315-318), but no runtime enforcement prevents binding to public interfaces.
